name: Toybox Bistro
content:
Toybox Bistro was a board game themed restaurant. During my only visit, I started checking down the menu for meat free options, becoming increasingly worried at the lack thereof, when I found the vegan section. “Ok, the food I eat doesn’t have to be vegan but I’ll take it,” I thought to myself, before quickly finding out that the vegan food section was really just a joke about how they didn’t have any vegan options. Now, if the restaurant was called “Big Al’s Juicy Steaks and Slaughterhouse” or the restaurant was explicitly meat-themed, I would laugh off the joke, order myself a lemonade, and enjoy the rest of my time there, but Toybox was in no way meat-themed at all (and no, you can’t be both board game and meat themed at the same time because that would be kinda gross and a health hazard). To make matters worse, I had a headache at the time, had been trapped in a car with a very loud person for the past hour leading up to that, and was stuck between two loud people having (in my opinion) the most pointless argument in the world. Luckily the two people were taller than me, so the argument narrowly missed me and most of the heat was safely concentrated about two inches above my head (see figure below).
Around a year later, one of my friends was eating here when they discovered that their “soup of the day” was a four-days-opened can of chili, triggering a food inspection that led to their closure. Although I know a couple of people that liked Toybox Bistro, I do not miss their absence.
